target.title: fix(sessions): preserve trajectory on reset for outbound forensics (fixes #90707)
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #90707.

On `/new` and `/reset`, OpenClaw archives the session transcript as
`<sid>.jsonl.reset.<ts>` but the sibling **trajectory** file
`<sid>.trajectory.jsonl` (the assistant's tool calls and tool results) and its
`<sid>.trajectory-path.json` pointer are lost — overwritten when the session
file path is reused, or left at their live names and then reclaimed by the
lifecycle cleanup for the now-unreferenced old session id. For sessions that
issued outbound communications (WhatsApp/email/SMS/Telegram), this makes
post-incident forensics impossible: after a wrong send there is no record of the
exact tool args or the tool result that preceded the send.

This change mirrors the transcript-archive behavior for the trajectory. In the
shared reset path `archiveSessionTranscriptsDetailed` (used by **both** reset
entry points — the auto-reply session rollover in
`src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts` and the gateway reset service in
`src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts`), when `reason === "reset"` the
trajectory runtime file and its pointer are renamed to `.reset.<ts>` archives
instead of being left to be deleted.

### Why this shape

- **Single owner.** Both reset flows funnel through
  `archiveSessionTranscriptsDetailed`, so one gated branch covers both rather
  than patching each call site.
- **No new retention code / no unbounded growth.** The reset retention sweep
  (`cleanupArchivedSessionTranscripts` →
  `parseSessionArchiveTimestamp(name, "reset")`) keys on the generic
  `.reset.<ts>` suffix, so `<sid>.trajectory.jsonl.reset.<ts>` and
  `<sid>.trajectory-path.json.reset.<ts>` are pruned by the existing
  `resetArchiveRetentionMs` rule automatically.
- **No re-deletion.** `isTrajectoryRuntimeArtifactName` requires an exact
  `.trajectory.jsonl` ending, so the archived form is no longer treated as a
  live trajectory and the existing `removeSessionTrajectoryArtifacts` cleanup
  leaves it alone.
- **Deleted/pruned sessions unchanged.** The branch is gated on
  `reason === "reset"`; `reason === "deleted"` still hands the trajectory to the
  existing removal path.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** A real session reset deleted the sibling
  `<sid>.trajectory.jsonl` and its `<sid>.trajectory-path.json` pointer while
  preserving the transcript; after this change both are archived as `.reset.<ts>`
  next to the transcript and survive the lifecycle cleanup.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux (Node 22),
  driving the actual production reset + cleanup functions against a real
  on-disk session directory (no stubs): `archiveSessionTranscriptsDetailed`
  (the function the gateway `/reset` service calls) followed by the real
  `removeSessionTrajectoryArtifacts` lifecycle cleanup.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Seed a real session dir with
  `<sid>.jsonl`, `<sid>.trajectory.jsonl` (recording a `wacli send` line), and
  `<sid>.trajectory-path.json`, then run `node --import tsx repro-90707.mjs`
  first on `origin/main`, then on this branch.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live console output below.

  On `origin/main` (current shipped behavior):

  ```
  BEFORE reset:                 [ '<sid>.jsonl', '<sid>.trajectory-path.json', '<sid>.trajectory.jsonl', 'sessions.json' ]
  AFTER real reset archive:     [ '<sid>.jsonl.reset.2026-06-18T14-11-18.709Z', '<sid>.trajectory-path.json', '<sid>.trajectory.jsonl', 'sessions.json' ]
  Lifecycle cleanup removed:    [ '<sid>.trajectory.jsonl', '<sid>.trajectory-path.json' ]
  FINAL on disk:                [ '<sid>.jsonl.reset.2026-06-18T14-11-18.709Z', 'sessions.json' ]
  RESULT: trajectory LOST
  ```

  On this branch:

  ```
  BEFORE reset:                 [ '<sid>.jsonl', '<sid>.trajectory-path.json', '<sid>.trajectory.jsonl', 'sessions.json' ]
  AFTER real reset archive:     [ '<sid>.jsonl.reset.2026-06-18T14-11-06.546Z', '<sid>.trajectory-path.json.reset.2026-06-18T14-11-06.546Z', '<sid>.trajectory.jsonl.reset.2026-06-18T14-11-06.546Z', 'sessions.json' ]
  Lifecycle cleanup removed:    []
  FINAL on disk:                [ '<sid>.jsonl.reset.2026-06-18T14-11-06.546Z', '<sid>.trajectory-path.json.reset.2026-06-18T14-11-06.546Z', '<sid>.trajectory.jsonl.reset.2026-06-18T14-11-06.546Z', 'sessions.json' ]
  RESULT: trajectory PRESERVED for forensics
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** On `origin/main` the trajectory and pointer are
  removed by the lifecycle cleanup (`RESULT: trajectory LOST`). On this branch
  both are archived as `.reset.<ts>` next to the transcript and the cleanup
  removes nothing (`RESULT: trajectory PRESERVED for forensics`).
- **What was not tested:** A full live gateway round-trip with a real model
  turn, and the `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_DIR` override placement (intentionally out
  of scope — the fix targets the default sibling placement described in the
  issue).

## Automated checks

Colocated regression cases were added in
`src/gateway/session-transcript-files.fs.archive-events.test.ts` (reset
preserves trajectory + pointer; a deleted reason leaves the live siblings for
the existing removal path). The full archive-events suite, the trajectory
cleanup / prune-integration suites, `tsgo:core` + `tsgo:core:test`, oxlint,
oxfmt, and `check:import-cycles` were all run locally and are green.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: pick-cat
